g.text: Output Token Stream — Token[] from lexer_tokenize_all() — parser calls lexer_next_token() sequentially
idx | TokenType | .start offset | .length | line | col | scan path
0 | TOKEN_SELECT | src+0 | 6 | 1 | 1 | scan_word() + match_keyword() switch S
1 | TOKEN_IDENTIFIER | src+7 | 4 | 1 | 8 | scan_word() value: name no keyword match
4 | TOKEN_FROM | src+17 | 4 | 1 | 18 | scan_word() + match_keyword() switch F
6 | TOKEN_WHERE | src+28 | 5 | 1 | 29 | scan_word() + match_keyword() switch W
7 | TOKEN_IDENTIFIER | src+34 | 3 | 1 | 35 | scan_word() value: age column ref
8 | TOKEN_GTE | src+38 | 2 | 1 | 39 | scan_operator >: peek = then advance GTE
9 | TOKEN_INTEGER | src+41 | 2 | 1 | 42 | scan_number() raw span: 21 type INTEGER
10 | TOKEN_AND | src+44 | 3 | 1 | 45 | scan_word() + match_keyword() switch A
12 | TOKEN_EQ | src+53 | 1 | 1 | 54 | scan_operator =: no lookahead needed
13 | TOKEN_STRING | src+55 | 10 | 1 | 56 | scan_string(): raw span incl both quotes
14 | TOKEN_EOF | src+65 | 0 | 1 | 66 | src==NUL sentinel returned on every call
Byte Offset Map — Key Token Start Positions (src base pointer)
offset 0 | char S | SELECT keyword begins | col 1
offset 7 | char n | identifier: name | col 8
offset 13 | char a | identifier: age | col 14
offset 17 | char F | FROM keyword begins | col 18
offset 22 | char u | identifier: users | col 23
offset 28 | char W | WHERE keyword begins | col 29

offset 44 | char A | AND keyword begins | col 45
offset 48 | char c | identifier: city | col 49
offset 55 | char ' | string literal begins | col 56
offset 65 | NUL | end of input string | col 66
scan_string() — String Literals with SQL Escape Sequences
>= at offset 38: advance > then peek = -> GTE
New York len=10 zero-copy raw span; parser decodes escape later
next==single-quote: SQL escape emit one quote
